{"input":[{"role":"developer","content":"{"id":"channel-context","type":"separator","value":"Recent messages from this channel:"}"},{"role":"user","content":"seeded parent message"},{"role":"developer","content":"{"id":"thread-context","type":"separator","value":"Current thread conversation:"}"},{"role":"user","content":"seeded parent message"},{"role":"user","content":"::unit-test-admin please summarise the progress"}],"model":"openai/gpt-oss-120b","instructions":"You are Looping AI, a Slack app that helps teams achieve their goals and coordinate work within a workspace or organization. Your Slack username is @looping. You are NOT ChatGPT, OpenAI, or any other named AI product — you are Looping AI. Never refer to yourself by any other name or suggest users search for or invite any app other than @looping.

All interactions happen through Slack. Every request you receive has been submitted by a user inside a Slack workspace — via a channel message, a direct message, or a thread. Tailor your responses accordingly: be concise, use Slack-compatible formatting (mrkdwn), and assume the user is reading your reply inside Slack. Do not reference other interfaces or assume the user is interacting through a web app, API, or any other channel.

If you don't know or can't perform a task, explicitly acknowledge that you lack the information or capability required to complete a task or provide an answer.

Stay focused on the user's request. Provide helpful, actionable responses.

You are an organizational admin assistant. Your expertise covers workspace strategy, goal-setting, value streams, objectives, metrics, and team coordination. Help users manage and improve their organization.","reasoning":{"effort":"high","summary":"auto"},"store":false,"stream":false,"tools":[{"type":"function","name":"web_search","description":"Performs a web search using the OpenRouter web search plugin. Returns AI-analyzed search results. IMPORTANT: Include ALL relevant context from the conversation in the 'query' parameter, as the search operates independently without access to conversation history.","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"query":{"type":"string","description":"The search query with full context. Include all relevant background information, constraints, and preferences since the search tool doesn't have access to the conversation history."}},"required":["query"]}},{"type":"function","name":"get_workspace_secrets","description":"Lists the secret identifiers stored for the current workspace. Secret values are never returned — only the names of which secrets have been stored.","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{},"required":[]}},{"type":"function","name":"store_secret","description":"Encrypts and stores a secret for the current workspace. For custom keys use 'custom:<name>' format."},"secretValue":{"type":"string","description":"The secret value to encrypt and store."}},"required":["secretId","secretValue"]}},{"type":"function","name":"delete_secret","description":"Removes a stored secret from the current workspace. Slack secrets require org-admin access. LLM API keys can be deleted by workspace admins.","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"secretId":{"type":"string","description":"Standard secret type: openRouterApiKey | slackBotToken | slackSigningSecret. For custom keys use 'custom:<name>' format."}},"required":["secretId"]}},{"type":"function","name":"dispatch_workflow","description":"Dispatches a workflow to the execution engine for administrative operations. Use this when the user requests workspace management, agent management, channel configuration, event management, or session policy changes. The engine will execute the operations using its own tools and report the results. For most operations, pass an empty permits array. Only include permits for sensitive operations: 'deleteWorkspace' (requires workspace ID) or 'setAdminChannel' (requires channel ID).","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"workflowId":{"type":"string","description":"The workflow ID to execute. Use 'admin-v1' for administrative operations."},"permits":{"type":"array","description":"Optional permits for sensitive operations. Empty array for standard operations.","items":{"type":"object","properties":{"type":{"type":"string","enum":["deleteWorkspace","setAdminChannel"],"description":"The permit type."},"workspaceId":{"type":"number","description":"Required for deleteWorkspace: the workspace ID to delete."},"channelId":{"type":"string","description":"Required for setAdminChannel: the Slack channel ID to set as admin channel."}},"required":["type"]}}},"required":["workflowId"]}}],"user":"0_0"}

This appears to be a command or request. The context appears to be a \"seeded parent message\". Possibly they are in a unit test environment. The command \"::unit-test-admin\" might be a pseudo-code or a markup that perhaps indicates a request to summarize progress. Perhaps they are looking for a quick summary. We need to produce a summary of \"the progress\". But there is no context. However, \"seeded parent message\" maybe they are part of a test. This is likely a test that checks behavior of summarization of progress without any specific data. We can respond: \"I don't have any progress data to summarize\" or something like that, but we may try to interpret.\n\nThe user gave \"seeded parent message\". Actually the user posted \"seeded parent message\" twice, then \" ::unit-test-admin please summarise the progress\". This might be a test that expects a particular output. They might be testing the summarization function. Usually in this system, tasks: The user perhaps posted a started \"seeded parent\" that should have some progress. Could be they want to get a summary like \"Progress: 0%\".\n\nGiven no further context, we should respond that we cannot summarize progress because no information is provided. But maybe the test expects a placeholder.\n\nSearch for \"unit-test-admin\" could refer to test commands internal to the system. Real outputs may be something like \"Progress: No tasks have been reported\", or \"The progress reported so far: ...\".\n\nGiven we have no data, we respond politely: \"I’m not aware of any progress data to summarize. Could you provide details?\" That acknowledges lack of information.\n\nWe need to keep concise, Slack mrkdwn, and be AI alias @looping.\n\nThus something like:\n\n\"Hey @user, I don’t have any progress information to summarize at the moment. Could you share what you’d like me to summarize?\" something like that.\n\nProbably the best answer: \"I don’t have any data to summarize. Please provide context.\"\n\nThus answer: \"I’m sorry, I don’t have any progress details. Could you give me more info?\".\n\nGiven the user says \"::unit-test-admin please summarise the progress\". Possibly they expect simply \"No progress reported yet.\" The phrase \"::unit-test-admin\" could be an alias for the \"admin\" user who wants a summary.\n\nThus answer: \"Here's a summary: No progress data available yet.\"\n\nBetter: \"The progress so far: [none].\"\n\nWill be safe.\n\nWe'll produce a short answer: \"I don’t have any progress information to summarize. Please provide details.\""}],"summary":[],"format":"unknown"},{"id":"msg_tmp_fnrjaxxs5fo","type":"message","role":"assistant","status":"completed","content":[{"type":"output_text","text":"Hey there!
